turns.speaker: User
turns.text: The paradox of free will is that the very act of deliberation presupposes it, yet neuroscience increasingly suggests that our sense of agency is a post-hoc narrative construction.

turns.speaker: Assistant
turns.text: Libet's experiments are often cited here, though the interpretation is contested. The readiness potential predating conscious intention doesn't necessarily dissolve agency—it might just mean the self extends deeper into neural process than we naively think.

turns.speaker: User
turns.text: That's a compatibilist move, isn't it? Dennett would approve. But I find it unsatisfying—it feels like renaming the problem.

scored_turn.speaker: User
scored_turn.text: The paradox of free will is that the very act of deliberation presupposes it, yet neuroscience increasingly suggests that our sense of agency is a post-hoc narrative construction.

scores.rationale: References empirical research and evidence-based claims.
High intellectual openness; engaging with complex, uncertain ideas.
